title: Oeceoclades gracilis
taxonomy: Oeceoclades gracilis is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Oeceoclades, which is a small group of terrestrial orchids. The plant is native to tropical regions of Africa and is often found in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Oeceoclades gracilis is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: angiosperms, clade: monocots, order: Asparagales, family: Orchidaceae, genus: Oeceoclades, species: O. gracilis. The species is further divided into two subspecies: O. g. gracilis and O. g. ruppii.

intro: Oeceoclades gracilis, also known as the gracile Oeceoclades, is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Oeceoclades gracilis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
